subject: Mathematics

lesson.title: MATRICES
topics: Overview: Matrices are an important part of Algebra because they provide an organised way of
arranging numbers, symbols, or expressions in rows and columns so that information can be
represented, analysed, and manipulated efficiently. They are widely used in Mathematics and in
everyday life to organise data such as school records, prices of goods, transport schedules, sports
activities:  Communication: Explain matrix concepts, notation, operations, and solutions clearly
using appropriate mathematical language and symbols.
 Creativity and Innovation: Apply matrices in representing real-life situations such as
timetables, stock records, transport fares, and test scores.
 Critical Thinking: Compare different matrix operations and determine the most suitable
method for solving a given problem.
 Digital Literacy: Use calculators, spreadsheets, and other digital tools to represent and
assessment: matrices and identify entries by their positions.
Learning Activity 2: Identifying different types of matrices (row, column, square,
Identifying different types of matrices helps learners classify matrices according to their structure.
